$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `background_jobs` repo (Phase-2, main
DB). One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-background-jobs-fixture.ts + background-jobs.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness background_jobs tier-2 test).
`background_jobs` is the durable work queue. It extends v4's
UserOwnedBaseRepository (a `userId` column) and has NO base-method override:
create/update/delete honor pinned id/createdAt/updatedAt. On top of CRUD it
adds the queue API exercised here: claimNextJob (atomic pick), markFailed
(exponential-backoff retry scheduling, DEAD-vs-FAILED branch), pause/resume,
and resetAllProcessingJobs (bulk PROCESSING -> DEAD).
COLUMNS / AFFINITIES: priority / attempts / maxAttempts are bare
z.number().default(N) -> REAL affinity (NOT INTEGER); the canonical dump's
js_number_to_json collapses the integer-valued REAL cell back to a JSON int.
payload is the open-JSON object column (z.record(string,unknown)) stored as
compact JSON; kept {}/single-key to avoid the multi-key key-order seam.
HARNESS FORM (minted-timestamp placeholder, like embedding_status): several
queue ops mint `now` (and markFailed mints `now + backoff`) UNCONDITIONALLY
from the system clock, so a pure zero-normalization form is impossible. ids
and createdAt are pinned and diffed EXACTLY; every deterministic column
(status, attempts, lastError, payload, priority, maxAttempts) is diffed
EXACTLY; only the four mintable timestamp columns (scheduledAt, startedAt,
completedAt, updatedAt) are collapsed to a `<ts>` placeholder on both sides.
createdAt is honored on create and preserved on update, so it stays exact.
DETERMINISM OF SELECTION: claimNextJob / resetStuckJobs depend on `now` vs
scheduledAt / startedAt. The seed pins those far in the past (2020) so the
claimable / stuck SET is the same no matter when the test runs.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key (the same one folders /
tags / text_replacement_rules use). NOT the real Friday pepper; safe to commit.

ops.comment: New PENDING job. Exercises the REAL number columns + open-JSON payload (single-key) on insert; id + timestamps pinned (createdAt diffed exact, scheduledAt/updatedAt placeholdered).
Atomic claim. Among the four PENDING/FAILED-and-eligible rows, seed A (priority 5) wins. Becomes PROCESSING, attempts 0->1 (exact), startedAt/updatedAt minted (placeholdered).
DEAD branch: seed C has attempts(3) >= maxAttempts(3), so status -> DEAD, lastError set (exact), scheduledAt = now+backoff (placeholdered). Status decision is deterministic and diffed exactly.
FAILED-retry branch: seed D has attempts(1) < maxAttempts(3), so status stays FAILED, lastError updated (exact), scheduledAt = now + min(30*2^1,300)=60s (placeholdered).
Pause seed B (PENDING -> PAUSED). updatedAt minted (placeholdered); status exact.
Resume seed B (PAUSED -> PENDING). scheduledAt = updatedAt = now (both placeholdered); status exact.
Bulk reset: the one PROCESSING row (seed A, claimed above) -> DEAD with the 'Orphaned on startup — killed' lastError (em-dash, exact). Count modified = 1.
Plain _update on the created job D: bump priority 2->7 (exact REAL) and set lastError (exact). updatedAt pinned in patch (still placeholdered by the harness form); createdAt preserved exact.
Fresh PROCESSING row K (created AFTER resetAllProcessingJobs so it doesn't change that op's count=1). markCompleted target next.

markCompleted WITHOUT a result: K -> COMPLETED, completedAt + updatedAt minted (placeholdered), payload {} untouched. NOTE: v4's SQLite backend throws 'no such column: payload.result' on the dotted-key result merge, so that path is unreachable on v4-on-SQLite and is NOT exercised here (the port's merge is a v5-only capability, covered by a unit test).
Fresh PROCESSING row J with a far-past startedAt — the resetStuckJobs target.
Stuck reset: J is the only PROCESSING row (K was completed), startedAt(2020) < now-60min, so J -> FAILED with lastError 'Timed out after 60 minutes' (exact). Count = 1.
Fresh PENDING row L — the cancel target.
Cancel L (PENDING -> DEAD) with lastError 'Cancelled by user' (exact). Returns true.
Fresh FAILED row M — the cancelByType target.
Cancel all non-completed EMBEDDING_REFIT jobs: M (FAILED) -> DEAD with lastError 'Superseded by new reindex' (exact). Count = 1.
Fresh COMPLETED row N — the deleteByTypesAndStatuses target (hard-deleted, so it must be absent from the final dump).
Hard-delete jobs matching type IN (LLM_LOG_CLEANUP) AND status IN (COMPLETED): row N is removed (absent from the final dump). Count = 1. Exercises the IN-clause placeholder building.
